name: Sunib Jagani
crispro_angle: CrisPRO can develop a predictive gene‑expression signature for cyclin E‑driven CDK2 dependence
hook: Speaker said: "tumors that have relatively high expression of cyclin E… do benefit… but PFS benefit is less"
email_subject: Your AACR 2026 work on PKMYT1 — a CrisPRO data angle

name: Yanrong Jiang
crispro_angle: CrisPRO's computational spine can integrate TCR repertoire dynamics with longitudinal ctDNA kinetics to create a high-resolution predictive model for non-operative management selection.
open_questions: Whether super molecular responders can be safely managed non-operatively.
hook: dynamic ctDNA clearance adds a strong discrimination layer beyond TCR alone, separating patients with similar immune profiles into distinct confidence regions of achieving response
email_subject: Your AACR 2026 work on WRN Helicase — a CrisPRO data angle

name: Francesca Vazquez
crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate MTAP deletion status with PRMT5 inhibitor response data to develop a predictive biomarker model.
hook: Speaker said: "We classified the cell lines into MTAP deleted or MTAP intact, and identified PRMT5 as a selective dependency in MTAP‑deleted cancers."
email_subject: Your AACR 2026 work on PRMT5/MTAP — a CrisPRO data angle

name: Zaman Ahmed
crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO's pathway simulation to identify synergistic immune checkpoint or STING agonist combos
hook: Speaker said: inhibitor increases gamma H2AX and activates cGAS‑STING pathway

name: Karina Patel

name: Dr. Timothy Yap

name: Joshua Luepe
crispro_angle: CrisPRO can benchmark the UIM platform against existing DNMTis using computational binding and off‑target prediction.
hook: Speaker highlighted that "the UIM is essential for DNA methylation maintenance" and that "this approach is not replication‑dependent".

name: Carmen Li
crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate ATAC‑seq and motif data to develop a predictive epigenetic risk score for BRCA1 carriers.

email_subject: Your AACR 2026 work on PARP Inhibitors — a CrisPRO data angle

name: John Ifraty
crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate multi‑modal embeddings and apply rigorous cross‑validation to improve predictive power beyond current AUROCs.
hook: Speaker noted: "the best predictor ... is MSI with an AUC of about 0.9, but for NRAS or PIK3CA it falls to 0.5‑0.6"

name: Saar Gill
crispro_angle: Computational identification of alternative entry receptors or metabolic modifiers to bypass the dNTP/LDLR requirement for resting T cells.
open_questions: Whether tissue-resident immune effector cells in lymphoid organs are better suited for cancer immunotherapy than circulating blood T cells.
The exact cause of early inflammatory toxicity in in vivo VLP delivery (innate immune response vs. CD3 cross-linking).
hook: successful reverse transcription requires a considerable dNTP pool, which is low in resting T cells

crispro_angle: CrisPRO can analyze the interplay of CCNE1 amplification, P16 expression, and CDK4/6 activity to refine patient stratification for MRT55811.

name: Luis A. Diaz Jr.
crispro_angle: Use computational modeling of tumor microenvironment and neoantigen landscape to flag patients for alternative combos.

name: Crystal Mackall
crispro_angle: Computational design of non-immunogenic humanized binders and junctional domains to eliminate T cell and B cell epitopes.
open_questions: Whether myeloid cells in the CNS contribute to a second axis of resistance.
Whether stealth platforms (used in allogeneic CARs) could prevent rejection in autologous CARs for solid tumors.
hook: these mapped in large part to the mouse-derived single-chain Fv... these data suggest that perhaps we should be using stealth platforms
